articles.title: Department of War Releases Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files in Historic Transparency Effort
articles.summary: Newly released Pentagon files include an internal FBI memo dated October 19, 1966 that reports witnesses in the 1960s saw three‑ to four‑foot‑tall beings in space suits and helmets landing from unidentified flying objects. S. Air Force withheld information to avoid mass panic, and described UFOs as polished metal objects that could move silently, hover, and emit bright lights.

en.title: Department of War Releases Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files in Historic Transparency Effort
en.summary: Newly released Pentagon files include an internal FBI memo dated October 19, 1966 that reports witnesses in the 1960s saw three‑ to four‑foot‑tall beings in space suits and helmets landing from unidentified flying objects. S. Air Force withheld information to avoid mass panic, and described UFOs as polished metal objects that could move silently, hover, and emit bright lights.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht nicht identifizierte Dateien zu anomalen Phänomenen im Rahmen historischer Transparenzbemühungen
de.summary: Zu den neu veröffentlichten Pentagon-Akten gehört ein internes FBI-Memo vom 19. Oktober 1966, in dem es heißt, Zeugen hätten in den 1960er Jahren drei bis vier Fuß große Wesen in Raumanzügen und Helmen gesehen, die von unbekannten Flugobjekten landeten. Die S. Air Force hielt Informationen zurück, um eine Massenpanik zu vermeiden, und beschrieb UFOs als polierte Metallobjekte, die sich lautlos bewegen, schweben und helles Licht ausstrahlen könnten.

articles.title: UFO Files Reveal … the Same Old Material
articles.summary: President Donald Trump and the Department of War released the UFO Files, which the author reviews and finds no new evidence of extraterrestrial visitation, only blurry photos, grainy videos, and artist reconstructions. The files include an artist sketch of an alleged bronze metallic object, a 2001 Georgian report of a Russian aircraft violation, a 2023 Mexican Congress UAP testimony, heavily redacted documents, and various photographs and videos from the southwestern United States, Japan, and Apollo missions.

en.title: UFO Files Reveal … the Same Old Material
en.summary: President Donald Trump and the Department of War released the UFO Files, which the author reviews and finds no new evidence of extraterrestrial visitation, only blurry photos, grainy videos, and artist reconstructions. The files include an artist sketch of an alleged bronze metallic object, a 2001 Georgian report of a Russian aircraft violation, a 2023 Mexican Congress UAP testimony, heavily redacted documents, and various photographs and videos from the southwestern United States, Japan, and Apollo missions.

de.title: UFO-Dateien enthüllen … das gleiche alte Material
de.summary: Präsident Donald Trump und das Kriegsministerium haben die UFO-Dateien veröffentlicht, die der Autor überprüft und keine neuen Beweise für außerirdische Besuche findet, sondern nur verschwommene Fotos, körnige Videos und Künstlerrekonstruktionen. Zu den Akten gehören eine Künstlerskizze eines angeblich bronzenen Metallobjekts, ein georgischer Bericht über einen russischen Flugzeugverstoß aus dem Jahr 2001, eine UAP-Aussage des mexikanischen Kongresses aus dem Jahr 2023, stark redigierte Dokumente sowie verschiedene Fotos und Videos aus dem Südwesten der USA, Japan und Apollo-Missionen.

articles.title: New documentary follows journalist Jeremy Corbell’s hunt for the truth on UFOs
articles.summary: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.

en.title: New documentary follows journalist Jeremy Corbell’s hunt for the truth on UFOs
en.summary: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.

de.title: Neuer Dokumentarfilm folgt der Suche des Journalisten Jeremy Corbell nach der Wahrheit über UFOs
de.summary: Der Journalist und Filmemacher Jeremy Corbell bespricht seinen neuen Dokumentarfilm mit NBC News-Korrespondent Gadi Schwartz. Der Dokumentarfilm trägt den Titel „Sleeping Dog“. Es untersucht Corbells Umgang mit Regierungsgeheimnissen. Es geht auch um Whistleblower und seine Suche nach der Wahrheit über UFOs.

articles.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
articles.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

en.title: Secret agent, who was about to blow the lid off UFO secrets, died 5 years ago. A whistleblower has now raised suspicion
en.summary: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released.

de.title: Der Geheimagent, der UFO-Geheimnisse enthüllen wollte, starb vor fünf Jahren. Ein Whistleblower hat nun Verdacht geäußert
de.summary: Kevin Childress, ein 30-jähriger Kriminalermittler des Energieministeriums, starb 2021 unerwartet in seinem Haus in Georgia, wobei die Behörden Komplikationen durch COVID-19 anführten. UFO-Whistleblower Luis Elizondo behauptet, Childress sei vor seinem Tod gesund gewesen und habe gewarnt, dass das DOE eine wichtige Rolle in UAP-Programmen gespielt habe. Elizondo sagt, das Energieministerium versuche ihn zum Schweigen zu bringen und stellt fest, dass weder eine öffentliche Autopsie noch eine detaillierte Todesursache veröffentlicht wurden.

summaries.file-release-department-of-war-releases-: Newly released Pentagon files include an internal FBI memo dated October 19, 1966 that reports witnesses in the 1960s saw three‑ to four‑foot‑tall beings in space suits and helmets landing from unidentified flying objects. The memo cites the book *Flying Saucers – Serious Business* by Frank Edwards, who claimed UFOs were space vehicles sent to observe Earth, that the U.S. Air Force withheld information to avoid mass panic, and described UFOs as polished metal objects that could move silently, hover, and emit bright lights. Witnesses named in the memo are described as reliable, including law enforcement officers, military personnel, and commercial airline pilots, and wreckage from at least three crashed objects—magnesium alloy, pure magnesium, and an unknown hard metal with 15‑micron spheres—has been recovered. The memo ends with Edwards predicting an imminent overt landing or deliberate contact by UFOs, and a trove of 162 UFO files, including photographs, video footage, and a 1947 flying‑disc sighting, was released to the public, with the Trump administration saying more documents will be released gradually.
summaries.ufo-files-reveal-the-same-old-material: President Donald Trump and the Department of War released the UFO Files, which the author reviews and finds no new evidence of extraterrestrial visitation, only blurry photos, grainy videos, and artist reconstructions. The files include an artist sketch of an alleged bronze metallic object, a 2001 Georgian report of a Russian aircraft violation, a 2023 Mexican Congress UAP testimony, heavily redacted documents, and various photographs and videos from the southwestern United States, Japan, and Apollo missions. The author notes that many of the images are ambiguous, often identified as drones, balloons, or optical phenomena, and that the files contain redactions protecting eyewitness identities and sensitive information. The author remains skeptical, stating that no definitive evidence of alien visitation has emerged from the release and that further evidence would be required to change that assessment.
summaries.film-new-documentary-follows-journalist-: Journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell discusses his new documentary with NBC News correspondent Gadi Schwartz. The documentary is titled “Sleeping Dog.” It explores Corbell’s navigation of government secrecy. It also covers whistleblowers and his search for truth about UFOs.
summaries.secret-agent-who-was-about-to-blow-the-l: Kevin Childress, a 30‑year Department of Energy criminal investigator, died unexpectedly in 2021 at his Georgia home, with authorities citing complications from COVID‑19. UFO whistleblower Luis Elizondo claims Childress was healthy before his death and had warned that the DOE had a significant role in UAP programs. Elizondo says the DOE is trying to silence him and notes that no public autopsy or detailed cause of death has been released. The FBI is investigating a series of missing and dead scientists linked to secret nuclear and UFO programs, including the disappearance of retired General William Neil McCasland. Other scientists, such as David Wilcock, have also died under suspicious circumstances, adding to the pattern of unexplained losses.

scanMeta.summaryPolicy: Only article-content summaries are app-eligible; metadata/headline fallback text is rejected later by summary_quality_gate.py.
